slug: 地球经历过比现在更热的时候_但那时候没有八十亿人
title: 地球经历过比现在更热的时候——但那时候没有八十亿人

outline.concept: Earth has been far hotter before — the PETM ~56 million years ago saw global temps 5-8°C above present, with crocodiles in the Arctic and palm trees in Antarctica. The planet and life don't inherently 'suffer' from warmth; given enough time, ecosystems reorganize and thrive. The 'crisis' framing isn't about temperature itself.
outline.query: 纪录片 恐龙 北极 温暖 古气候 化石

outline.concept: The critical variable is rate of change, not absolute temperature. The PETM's massive carbon release unfolded over ~20,000 years; current forcing is on a ~200-year timescale. Species can migrate or evolve given millennia, but when climate zones shift faster than trees can seed poleward, you get ecological whiplash — not gentle reorganization.
outline.query: 物种 灭绝 来不及 迁移 适应 变化太快

outline.concept: Human infrastructure is spatially locked to Holocene stability — coastal megacities at current sea levels, grain belts at current rain bands, reservoirs at current snowpack patterns. Unlike other species that can gradually shift range, a city of 20 million cannot simply 'migrate' 300km north over a decade.
outline.query: 洪水 城市 搬不走 基建 海平面 农田 干旱

outline.concept: Population density + global interdependence creates cascade risk that has no paleo-analog. When 8 billion people depend on synchronized supply chains — a wheat failure in one region spikes bread prices in forty countries — a climate shock doesn't just displace; it propagates through systems that didn't exist in any past warm period.
outline.query: 粮价 涨 供应链 断 短缺 连锁反应 依赖
